hypothesis: A coupled ensemble composed of heterogeneous members (different GNN architectures and independent bootstrap minibatch sampling) trained with consensus targets drawn from an external unlabeled chemical library will yield an individual model that achieves significantly higher out‑of‑domain test accuracy and lower expected calibration error on held‑out external molecular datasets than an equally sized homogeneous ensemble (same architecture and shared minibatches) trained with the same semi‑supervised consensus protocol.
